verses.text: karmanye vadhikaraste ma phaleshu kadacana
verses.meaning: You have a right to perform your prescribed duties, but you are not entitled to the fruits of your actions. Never consider yourself the cause of the results of your activities, nor be attached to inaction.
verses.context: Krishna responds to Arjuna's despair about fighting his kinsmen
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: yogasthah kuru karmani sangam tyaktva dhananjaya
verses.meaning: Perform your duty equipoised, O Arjuna, abandoning all attachment to success or failure. Such equanimity is called yoga.
verses.context: Krishna continues instructing Arjuna on performing duty with balance
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: buddhiyukto jahatiha ubhe sukrta duskrte
verses.meaning: Endowed with such a faith, he seeks the favor of a particular demigod and obtains his desires. But in reality these benefits are bestowed by Me alone.
verses.context: Krishna explains how one with wisdom transcends good and bad karma
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: tasmad asaktah satatam karyam karma samacara
verses.meaning: Therefore, without attachment, perform your duty, for by working without attachment one attains the Supreme.
verses.context: Krishna emphasizes selfless action as the path to liberation
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: yada yada hi dharmasya glanir bhavati bharata
verses.meaning: Whenever and wherever there is a decline in religious practice, O descendant of Bharata, and a predominant rise of irreligion — at that time I descend Myself.
verses.context: Krishna reveals His divine mission to restore cosmic order
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: paritranaya sadhunam vinasaya ca duskrtam
verses.meaning: To deliver the pious and to annihilate the miscreants, as well as to reestablish the principles of religion, I Myself appear, millennium after millennium.
verses.context: Krishna explains the purpose of His descent into the world
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: bhoktaram yajnatapasam sarvaloka mahesvaram
verses.meaning: The sages, knowing Me as the ultimate purpose of all sacrifices and austerities, the Supreme Lord of all worlds, and the friend of all living entities, attain peace.
verses.context: Krishna describes the path to peace through recognizing His supremacy
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: uddhared atmanatmanam natmanam avasadayet
verses.meaning: One must deliver himself with the help of his mind, and not degrade himself. The mind is the friend of the conditioned soul, and his enemy as well.
verses.context: Krishna instructs on self-mastery through mental discipline
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: bandhur atmatmanas tasya yenatmaivatmana jitah
verses.meaning: For him who has conquered the mind, the mind is the best of friends; but for one who has failed to do so, his mind will remain the greatest enemy.
verses.context: Krishna explains the dual nature of the mind as friend or foe
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: asamsayam maha-baho mano durnigraham calam
verses.meaning: O mighty-armed son of Kunti, it is undoubtedly very difficult to curb the restless mind, but it is possible by suitable practice and by detachment.
verses.context: Krishna answers Arjuna's concern about the restless mind
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: mattah parataram nanyat kincid asti dhananjaya
verses.meaning: O conqueror of wealth, there is no truth superior to Me. Everything rests upon Me, as pearls are strung on a thread.
verses.context: Krishna reveals Himself as the ultimate source and foundation of all
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: bahunam janmanam ante jnanavan mam prapadyate
verses.meaning: After many births and deaths, he who is actually in knowledge surrenders unto Me, knowing Me to be the cause of all causes and all that is.
verses.context: Krishna describes the rare soul who reaches full surrender
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: ananyas cintayanto mam ye janah paryupasate
verses.meaning: But those who always worship Me with exclusive devotion, meditating on My transcendental form — to them I carry what they lack, and I preserve what they have.
verses.context: Krishna promises to care for those who worship Him with devotion
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: yat karosi yad asnasi yaj juhosi dadasi yat
verses.meaning: Whatever you do, whatever you eat, whatever you offer or give away, and whatever austerities you perform — do that, O son of Kunti, as an offering to Me.
verses.context: Krishna instructs how to transform daily activities into worship
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: man-mana bhava mad-bhakto mad-yaji mam namaskuru
verses.meaning: Engage your mind always in thinking of Me, become My devotee, offer obeisances to Me, and worship Me. Being completely absorbed in Me, surely you will come to Me.
verses.context: Krishna gives the essence of spiritual practice in four instructions
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: aham sarvasya prabhavo mattah sarvam pravartate
verses.meaning: I am the source of all spiritual and material worlds. Everything emanates from Me. The wise who perfectly know this engage in My devotional service.
verses.context: Krishna reveals His position as the origin of all existence
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: advesta sarva-bhutanam maitrah karuna eva ca
verses.meaning: One who is not envious but is a kind friend to all living entities, who does not think himself a proprietor and is free from false ego, who is equal in both happiness and distress...
verses.context: Krishna describes the qualities dear to Him in His devotees
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: ye tu dharmamrtam idam yathoktam paryupasate
verses.meaning: Those who follow this imperishable path of devotional service and who completely engage themselves with faith, making Me the supreme goal, are very, very dear to Me.
verses.context: Krishna declares His special affection for those on the path of devotion
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: amanitvam adambhitvam ahimsa ksantir arjavam
verses.meaning: Humility; pridelessness; nonviolence; tolerance; simplicity; approaching a bona fide spiritual master; cleanliness; steadiness; self-control...
verses.context: Krishna lists the qualities that constitute true knowledge
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: mamaivamso jiva-loke jiva-bhutah sanatanah
verses.meaning: The living entities in this conditioned world are My eternal, fragmental parts. Due to the conditioned life, they are struggling very hard with the six senses, which include the mind.
verses.context: Krishna explains the eternal relationship between the soul and the Supreme
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: trividham narakasyedam dvaram nasanam atmanah
verses.meaning: There are three gates leading to this hell — lust, anger and greed. Every sane man should give these up, for they lead to the degradation of the soul.
verses.context: Krishna warns against the three doors to spiritual destruction
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: sattvanurupa sarvasya sraddha bhavati bharata
verses.meaning: O son of Bharata, according to one's existence under the various modes of nature, one evolves a particular kind of faith. The living being is said to be of a particular faith according to the modes he has acquired.
verses.context: Krishna explains how one's faith corresponds to their nature
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: man-mana bhava mad-bhakto mad-yaji mam namaskuru
verses.meaning: Always think of Me, become My devotee, worship Me and offer your homage unto Me. Thus you will come to Me without fail. I promise you this because you are My very dear friend.
verses.context: Krishna gives His intimate instruction and promise to Arjuna
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: sarva-dharman parityajya mam ekam saranam vraja
verses.meaning: Abandon all varieties of religion and just surrender unto Me. I shall deliver you from all sinful reactions. Do not fear.
verses.context: Krishna's ultimate instruction: surrender unto Him alone
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: sri-bhagavan uvaca asocyan anvasocas tvam prajna-vadams ca bhasase
verses.meaning: The Supreme Personality of Godhead said: While speaking learned words, you are mourning for what is not worthy of grief. Those who are wise lament neither for the living nor for the dead.
verses.context: Krishna begins His teaching by addressing Arjuna's misplaced grief
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: dehino smin yatha dehe kaumaram yauvanam jara
verses.meaning: As the embodied soul continuously passes, in this body, from boyhood to youth to old age, the soul similarly passes into another body at death. A sober person is not bewildered by such a change.
verses.context: Krishna explains the soul's journey through different bodies
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: matra-sparas tu kaunteya sitosna-sukha-duhkha-dah
verses.meaning: O son of Kunti, the nonpermanent appearance of happiness and distress, and their disappearance in due course, are like the appearance and disappearance of winter and summer seasons. They arise from sense perception, O scion of Bharata, and one must learn to tolerate them without being disturbed.
verses.context: Krishna teaches Arjuna to tolerate dualities with equanimity
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: yam hi na vyathayanty ete purusam purusarsabha
verses.meaning: O best among men [Arjuna], the person who is not disturbed by happiness and distress and is steady in both is certainly eligible for liberation.
verses.context: Krishna describes the qualification for liberation from material existence
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: na jayate mriyate va kadacin nayam bhutva bhavita va na bhuyah
verses.meaning: For the soul there is neither birth nor death at any time. He has not come into being, does not come into being, and will not come into being. He is unborn, eternal, ever-existing and primeval. He is not slain when the body is slain.
verses.context: Krishna reveals the eternal nature of the individual soul
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: vasamsi jirnani yatha vihaya navani grhnati naro parani
verses.meaning: As a person puts on new garments, giving up old ones, the soul similarly accepts new material bodies, giving up the old and useless ones.
verses.context: Krishna uses the analogy of changing clothes to explain reincarnation
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: dhritarashtra uvaca dharmakshetre kurukshetre samaveta yuyutsavah
verses.meaning: Dhritarashtra said: O Sanjaya, after assembling in the place of pilgrimage at Kurukshetra, what did my sons and the sons of Pandu do, being desirous to fight?
verses.context: The blind king Dhritarashtra asks Sanjaya about the events on the battlefield
verses.speaker: Dhritarashtra

verses.text: arjuna uvaca drstvemam svajanam krishna yuyutsum samupasthitam
verses.meaning: Arjuna said: O Krishna, seeing my own kinsmen arrayed and eager to fight, my limbs quail, my mouth becomes dry, my body trembles, and my hair stands on end.
verses.context: Arjuna describes his physical and emotional distress upon seeing his relatives ready for battle
verses.speaker: Arjuna

verses.text: kim no rajyena govinda kim bhogair jivitena va
verses.meaning: Of what avail is a kingdom to us, O Govinda? Of what avail are enjoyments or even life itself?
verses.context: Arjuna questions the value of victory if it means destroying his own kinsmen
verses.speaker: Arjuna

verses.text: kula-kshaye pranasyanti kula-dharmah sanatanah
verses.meaning: With the destruction of the dynasty, the eternal family tradition is vanquished, and thus the rest of the family becomes involved in irreligion.
verses.context: Arjuna argues that war would destroy the family traditions that maintain dharma
verses.speaker: Arjuna

verses.text: sanjaya uvaca evam uktvaarjunah sankhye rathopastha upavisat
verses.meaning: Sanjaya said: Having thus spoken in the midst of the battlefield, Arjuna cast aside his bow and arrows and sat down on the chariot, his mind overwhelmed with grief.
verses.context: Sanjaya narrates Arjuna's complete breakdown before the battle begins
verses.speaker: Sanjaya

verses.text: karpanya-dosopahata-svabhavah prcchami tvam dharma-sammudha-cetah
verses.meaning: My heart is overpowered by the taint of pity. My mind is confused as to duty. I ask Thee: tell me decisively what is good for me. I am Thy disciple. Instruct me who has taken refuge in Thee.
verses.context: Arjuna formally surrenders to Krishna as a disciple and asks for guidance
verses.speaker: Arjuna

verses.text: avinasi tu tad viddhi yena sarvam idam tatam
verses.meaning: Know that to be indestructible by which all this is pervaded. None can cause the destruction of that — the imperishable.
verses.context: Krishna teaches that the soul which pervades the body cannot be destroyed
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: jatasya hi dhruvo mrtyur dhruvam janma mrtasya ca
verses.meaning: For that which is born, death is certain; and for that which is dead, birth is certain. Therefore, you should not grieve over the inevitable.
verses.context: Krishna explains the cycle of birth and death as a natural certainty
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: dhyayato vishayan pumsah sangas tesupajayate
verses.meaning: While contemplating the objects of the senses, a person develops attachment for them, and from such attachment lust develops, and from lust anger arises.
verses.context: Krishna describes the chain reaction from sense contemplation to spiritual downfall
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: krodhad bhavati sammohah sammohat smrti-vibhramah
verses.meaning: From anger, complete delusion arises, and from delusion bewilderment of memory. When memory is bewildered, intelligence is lost, and when intelligence is lost one falls down again into the material pool.
verses.context: Krishna continues explaining the chain of spiritual degradation
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: prajahati yada kaman sarvan partha manogatan
verses.meaning: The Supreme Personality of Godhead said: O Partha, when a man gives up all varieties of desire for sense gratification, which arise from mental concoction, and when his mind, thus purified, finds satisfaction in the self alone, then he is said to be in pure transcendental consciousness.
verses.context: Krishna defines the person of steady wisdom in response to Arjuna's question
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: jnana-yogena sankhyanam karma-yogena yoginam
verses.meaning: In this world there is a twofold path, as I said before, O sinless one — the path of knowledge for the contemplative, and the path of action for the active.
verses.context: Krishna explains the two spiritual paths suited to different temperaments
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: na hi kascit ksanam api jatu tisthaty akarma-krt
verses.meaning: Verily, none can ever remain even for a moment without performing action; for everyone is made to act helplessly by the qualities born of prakriti (nature).
verses.context: Krishna explains that total inaction is impossible due to the forces of nature
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: yajnarthat karmano nyatra loko yam karma-bandhanah
verses.meaning: Work done as a sacrifice for Vishnu has to be performed; otherwise work causes bondage in this material world. Therefore, O son of Kunti, perform your prescribed duties for His satisfaction, and in that way you will always remain free from bondage.
verses.context: Krishna teaches that work dedicated to the Lord frees one from karmic bondage
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: yad yad acarati sreshtas tat tad evetaro janah
verses.meaning: Whatever a great man does, that other men also do. Whatever standard he sets up, the generality of men follow.
verses.context: Krishna explains how the actions of great leaders set the standard for society
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: prakrteh kriyamanani gunaih karmani sarvasah
verses.meaning: All actions are wrought in all cases by the qualities of nature only. He whose mind is deluded by egoism thinks, 'I am the doer.'
verses.context: Krishna reveals that it is nature's modes, not the self, that perform all actions
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: mayi sarvani karmani sannyasyadhyatma-cetasa
verses.meaning: Surrendering all actions to Me, with the mind intent on the Self, free from longing and selfishness, fight — free from mental fever.
verses.context: Krishna instructs Arjuna to offer all actions to Him and fight without anxiety
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: sreyan sva-dharmo vigunah para-dharmat svanusthitat
verses.meaning: Better is one's own dharma, though imperfectly performed, than the dharma of another well performed. Better is death in the performance of one's own dharma, for the dharma of another is fraught with danger.
verses.context: Krishna emphasizes the importance of following one's own nature and duty
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: kama esa krodha esa rajo-guna-samudbhavah
verses.meaning: It is desire, it is anger born of the rajo-guna (quality of passion), all-sinful and all-devouring; know this as the foe here in this world.
verses.context: Krishna identifies desire and anger as the greatest enemies of the spiritual aspirant
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: indriyani parany ahur indriyebhyah param manah
verses.meaning: The senses are said to be superior to the body; the mind is superior to the senses; the intellect is superior to the mind; and that which is superior to the intellect is He, the Self.
verses.context: Krishna describes the hierarchy from senses to the Supreme Self
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: evam parampara-praptam imam rajarsayo viduh
verses.meaning: This supreme science was thus received through the chain of disciplic succession, and the saintly kings understood it in that way. But in course of time the succession was broken, and therefore the science as it is appears to be lost.
verses.context: Krishna explains how the knowledge of yoga was passed down through disciplic succession
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: janma karma ca me divyam evam yo vetti tattvatah
verses.meaning: One who knows the transcendental nature of My appearance and activities does not, upon leaving the body, take his birth again in this material world, but attains My eternal abode, O Arjuna.
verses.context: Krishna reveals that understanding His divine nature leads to liberation
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: karmany akarma yah pasyed akarmani ca karma yah
verses.meaning: He who recognises inaction in action and action in inaction is wise among men; he is a yogi and a true performer of all actions.
verses.context: Krishna teaches the subtle understanding of action and inaction
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: brahmarpanam brahma havir brahmagnau brahmana hutam
verses.meaning: Brahman is the offering, Brahman is the oblation, poured out by Brahman into the fire of Brahman; Brahman is to be attained by him who always sees Brahman in action.
verses.context: Krishna describes how the enlightened see the divine in every act of offering
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: sreyan dravyamayad yajnaj jnana-yajnah parantapa
verses.meaning: Superior is the sacrifice of knowledge to the sacrifice of material possessions, O scorcher of foes. All actions in their entirety, O Partha, culminate in knowledge.
verses.context: Krishna declares the superiority of the sacrifice of knowledge over material offerings
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: tad viddhi pranipatena pariprasnena sevaya
verses.meaning: Learn that by prostration, by question and by service; the wise, the seers of the Truth, will instruct thee in that knowledge.
verses.context: Krishna advises approaching a spiritual teacher with humility and service to gain knowledge
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: na hi jnanena sadrsam pavitram iha vidyate
verses.meaning: Verily there is no purifier in this world like knowledge. He who is perfected in yoga finds it in the Self in course of time.
verses.context: Krishna declares that nothing in this world purifies like spiritual knowledge
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: jneyah sa nitya-sannyasi yo na dvesti na kanksati
verses.meaning: He should be known as a perpetual sannyasi (renunciate) who neither hates nor desires; for, free from the pairs of opposites, O mighty-armed, he is easily set free from bondage.
verses.context: Krishna defines true renunciation as freedom from likes and dislikes
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: yoga-yukto visuddhatma vijitatma jitendriyah
verses.meaning: He who is devoted to the path of action, whose mind is quite pure, who has conquered the self, who has subdued his senses, whose self becomes the Self of all beings — he is not tainted though he acts.
verses.context: Krishna describes how the pure-hearted yogi remains untouched by action
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: brahmany adhaya karmani sangam tyaktva karoti yah
verses.meaning: He who performs actions, offering them to Brahman and abandoning attachment, is not tainted by sin, just as a lotus leaf is untouched by water.
verses.context: Krishna uses the lotus analogy to describe the detached worker
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: nadatte kasyacit papam na caiva sukrtam vibhuh
verses.meaning: The Lord takes neither the demerit nor even the merit of any. Knowledge is enveloped by ignorance; thereby beings are deluded.
verses.context: Krishna explains that the Lord does not accept anyone's sin or virtue; ignorance causes delusion
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: vidya-vinaya-sampanne brahmane gavi hastini
verses.meaning: The humble sages, by virtue of true knowledge, see with equal vision a learned and gentle brahmana, a cow, an elephant, a dog and an outcaste.
verses.context: Krishna describes the equal vision of the truly wise who see the Self in all beings
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: ye hi samsparsaja bhoga duhkha-yonaya eva te
verses.meaning: The pleasures that are born of sense-contacts are verily sources of pain only, for they have a beginning and an end, O son of Kunti. The wise man does not rejoice in them.
verses.context: Krishna warns that sense pleasures are temporary and ultimately lead to suffering
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: jitatmanah prasantasya paramatma samahitah
verses.meaning: The Supreme Self of him who is self-controlled and peaceful is balanced in cold and heat, pleasure and pain, as also in honour and dishonour.
verses.context: Krishna describes the balanced state of one who has mastered the self
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: yuktahara-viharasya yukta-cestasya karmasu
verses.meaning: For one who is moderate in eating and recreation, who is moderate in exertion in actions, who is moderate in sleep and wakefulness, yoga becomes the destroyer of pain.
verses.context: Krishna prescribes moderation in all aspects of life as the key to successful yoga
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: yatha dipo nivata-stho nengate sopama smrta
verses.meaning: As a lamp in a windless place does not flicker — to such is compared the yogi of controlled mind, practising yoga in the Self.
verses.context: Krishna uses the analogy of a steady flame to describe the concentrated mind in meditation
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: yato yato niscarati manas cancalam asthiram
verses.meaning: From whatever cause the restless and unsteady mind wanders away, from that let him restrain it and bring it back under the control of the Self alone.
verses.context: Krishna gives practical instruction on how to deal with a wandering mind during meditation
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: sarva-bhuta-stham atmanam sarva-bhutani catmani
verses.meaning: With the mind harmonised by yoga, he sees the Self abiding in all beings and all beings in the Self; he sees the same everywhere.
verses.context: Krishna describes the vision of oneness attained through yoga
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: partha naiveha namutra vinasas tasya vidyate
verses.meaning: O son of Pritha, neither in this world nor in the next world is there destruction for him; none, verily, who does good, O My son, ever comes to grief.
verses.context: Krishna assures Arjuna that no spiritual effort is ever wasted
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: bhumir apo nalo vayuh kham mano buddhir eva ca
verses.meaning: Earth, water, fire, air, ether, mind, intellect and egoism — thus is My nature divided eightfold.
verses.context: Krishna describes the eight components of His material energy
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: raso ham apsu kaunteya prabhasmi sasi-suryayoh
verses.meaning: O son of Kunti, I am the taste of water, the light of the sun and the moon, the syllable Om in all the Vedas, sound in ether and ability in man.
verses.context: Krishna reveals how He is present as the essential quality in all things
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: daivi hy esa guna-mayi mama maya duratyaya
verses.meaning: Verily, this divine illusion of Mine, made up of the three qualities of nature, is difficult to cross over. Those who take refuge in Me alone cross over this illusion.
verses.context: Krishna explains that His maya is hard to overcome, but surrender to Him is the way
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: catur-vidha bhajante mam janah sukrtino rjuna
verses.meaning: Four kinds of virtuous men worship Me, O Arjuna — the distressed, the seeker of knowledge, the seeker of wealth, and the wise, O lord of the Bharatas.
verses.context: Krishna describes the four types of pious people who turn to Him
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: vedaham samatitani vartamanani carjuna
verses.meaning: O Arjuna, I know the beings of the past, the present and the future, but Me no one knows.
verses.context: Krishna declares His knowledge of all beings while remaining beyond the reach of ordinary understanding
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: anta-kale ca mam eva smaran muktva kalevaram
verses.meaning: And whoever, at the end of his life, quits his body remembering Me alone, at once attains My nature. Of this there is no doubt.
verses.context: Krishna teaches that remembering Him at the time of death leads to liberation
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: yam yam vapi smaran bhavam tyajaty ante kalevaram
verses.meaning: Whatever state of being one remembers when he quits his body, O son of Kunti, that state he will attain without fail.
verses.context: Krishna explains that the final thought at death determines one's next destination
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: tasmat sarvesu kalesu mam anusmara yudhya ca
verses.meaning: Therefore, at all times, remember Me only and fight. With mind and intellect fixed on Me, thou shalt doubtless come to Me alone.
verses.context: Krishna advises constant remembrance of Him while performing one's worldly duties
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: kavim puranam anusasitaram anor aniyamsam anusmared yah
verses.meaning: He who meditates on the Omniscient, the Ancient, the Ruler, smaller than the atom, the sustainer of all, of inconceivable form, luminous like the sun, beyond the darkness of ignorance...
verses.context: Krishna describes the qualities to meditate upon for attaining the Supreme at the time of death
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: ananya-cetah satatam yo mam smarati nityasah
verses.meaning: For one who always remembers Me without deviation, I am easy to obtain, O son of Pritha, because of his constant engagement in devotional service.
verses.context: Krishna assures that He is easily attainable for the ever-devoted
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: abrahma-bhuvanal lokah punar avartino rjuna
verses.meaning: From the highest planet in the material world down to the lowest, all are places of misery wherein repeated birth and death take place. But one who attains to My abode, O son of Kunti, never takes birth again.
verses.context: Krishna contrasts the temporary material worlds with His eternal abode
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: paras tasmat tu bhavo nyo vyakto vyaktat sanatanah
verses.meaning: But verily there exists, higher than this unmanifested, another unmanifested Eternal which is not destroyed when all beings are destroyed.
verses.context: Krishna describes the supreme unmanifest reality beyond the material creation
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: raja-vidya raja-guhyam pavitram idam uttamam
verses.meaning: This is the kingly science, the kingly secret, the supreme purifier, realisable by direct intuitional knowledge, according to dharma, very easy to perform and imperishable.
verses.context: Krishna introduces the most confidential knowledge as the king of all learning
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: maya tatam idam sarvam jagad avyakta-murtina
verses.meaning: All this world is pervaded by Me in My unmanifest form. All beings exist in Me, but I do not dwell in them.
verses.context: Krishna reveals His all-pervading yet transcendent nature
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: aham kratur aham yajnah svadhaham aham ausadham
verses.meaning: I am the Vedic ritual, I am the sacrifice, I am the offering to the ancestors, I am the medicinal herb, I am the mantra, I am the clarified butter, I am the fire and I am the act of offering.
verses.context: Krishna declares Himself as every aspect of the sacred ritual
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: patram pushpam phalam toyam yo me bhaktya prayacchati
verses.meaning: Whoever offers Me with devotion a leaf, a flower, a fruit or water — I accept that, the pious offering of the pure in heart.
verses.context: Krishna shows that sincere devotion matters more than the grandeur of the offering
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: samo ham sarva-bhutesu na me dvesyo sti na priyah
verses.meaning: I am the same to all beings. To Me there is none hateful or dear. But those who worship Me with devotion are in Me, and I am also in them.
verses.context: Krishna declares His impartiality while affirming the special bond with His devotees
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: api cet su-duracaro bhajate mam ananya-bhak
verses.meaning: Even if the most sinful person worships Me with exclusive devotion, he is to be considered righteous, for he has rightly resolved.
verses.context: Krishna offers hope to all, declaring that even a great sinner can be redeemed through sincere devotion
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: tesam satata-yuktanam bhajatam priti-purvakam
verses.meaning: To those who are constantly devoted and who worship Me with love, I give the understanding by which they can come to Me.
verses.context: Krishna promises to bestow spiritual insight upon His loving devotees
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: tesam evanukampartham aham ajnana-jam tamah
verses.meaning: Out of compassion for them, I, dwelling in their hearts, destroy with the shining lamp of knowledge the darkness born of ignorance.
verses.context: Krishna describes how He personally dispels the ignorance of His devotees
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: aham atma gudakesa sarva-bhutasaya-sthitah
verses.meaning: I am the Self, O Gudakesa, seated in the hearts of all beings. I am the beginning, the middle and the end of all beings.
verses.context: Krishna begins enumerating His divine glories by declaring Himself the Self in all
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: vedanam sama-vedo smi devanam asmi vasavah
verses.meaning: Of the Vedas I am the Sama Veda; of the demigods I am Vasava (Indra); of the senses I am the mind; and in living beings I am the living force (consciousness).
verses.context: Krishna identifies Himself with the best among various categories of creation
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: aksaranam akaro smi dvandvah samasikasya ca
verses.meaning: Of letters I am the letter A, and among compound words I am the dual compound. I am also inexhaustible time. Of creators I am Brahma.
verses.context: Krishna continues revealing His vibhutis — the divine glories pervading creation
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: yad yad vibhutimat sattvam srimad urjitam eva va
verses.meaning: Whatever being there is that is glorious, prosperous or powerful, know that to be a manifestation of a part of My splendour.
verses.context: Krishna summarizes that all greatness in the world is but a spark of His infinite glory
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: sanjaya uvaca evam uktva tato rajan maha-yogesvaro harih
verses.meaning: Sanjaya said: Having spoken thus, O King, the great Lord of Yoga, Hari (Krishna), showed to Arjuna His supreme cosmic form.
verses.context: Sanjaya narrates the moment Krishna reveals His universal form to Arjuna
verses.speaker: Sanjaya

verses.text: divi surya-sahasrasya bhaved yugapad utthita
verses.meaning: If the splendour of a thousand suns were to blaze out simultaneously in the sky, that would be the splendour of that mighty Being.
verses.context: Sanjaya describes the overwhelming brilliance of Krishna's universal form
verses.speaker: Sanjaya

verses.text: aneka-bahudara-vaktra-netram pasyami tvam sarvato nanta-rupam
verses.meaning: I see Thee of boundless form on every side, with many arms, stomachs, mouths and eyes; neither the end nor the middle nor the beginning do I see, O Lord of the universe, O cosmic form.
verses.context: Arjuna describes the overwhelming vastness of Krishna's universal form
verses.speaker: Arjuna

verses.text: kalo smi loka-ksaya-krt pravrddho lokan samahartum iha pravrttah
verses.meaning: I am Time, the mighty destroyer of the world, out here to destroy. Even without your participation, all the warriors standing arrayed in the opposing armies shall cease to exist.
verses.context: Krishna reveals Himself as all-devouring Time, assuring Arjuna that the outcome is already determined
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: tasmat tvam uttistha yaso labhasva jitva satrun bhunksva rajyam samrddham
verses.meaning: Therefore, stand up and obtain fame. Conquer the enemies and enjoy the flourishing kingdom. They are already put to death by Me; you, O Savyasachin, are merely an instrument.
verses.context: Krishna tells Arjuna to be His instrument and fight, as the enemies are already slain by divine will
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: sthane hrsikesa tava prakirtya jagat prahrsyaty anurajyate ca
verses.meaning: It is meet, O Hrishikesa, that the world delights and rejoices in Thy praise; the demons fly in fear to all quarters and the hosts of Siddhas bow to Thee.
verses.context: Arjuna offers prayers and praise after witnessing the cosmic form
verses.speaker: Arjuna

verses.text: mat-karma-krn mat-paramo mad-bhaktah sanga-varjitah
verses.meaning: He who does all actions for Me, who looks upon Me as the Supreme, who is devoted to Me, who is free from attachment, who bears enmity towards no creature — he comes to Me, O Pandava.
verses.context: Krishna summarizes the essence of devotion after withdrawing His cosmic form
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: mayyavesya mano ye mam nitya-yukta upasate
verses.meaning: Those who, fixing their minds on Me, worship Me, ever steadfast and endowed with supreme faith — these are the best in yoga in My opinion.
verses.context: Krishna declares that devotees who worship His personal form with faith are the highest yogis
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: mayy eva mana adhatsva mayi buddhim nivesaya
verses.meaning: Fix your mind on Me only, place your intellect in Me; then you shall no doubt live in Me alone hereafter.
verses.context: Krishna gives the highest instruction: fix both mind and intellect on Him
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: abhyase py asamartho si mat-karma-paramo bhava
verses.meaning: If you are unable even to practise, be thou intent on doing actions for My sake; even by doing actions for My sake, thou shalt attain perfection.
verses.context: Krishna offers a step-down approach for those who cannot meditate — serve Him through action
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: sreyo hi jnanam abhyasaj jnanad dhyanam visisyate
verses.meaning: Better indeed is knowledge than practice; better than knowledge is meditation; better than meditation is the renunciation of the fruit of actions; peace immediately follows renunciation.
verses.context: Krishna ranks the spiritual practices, declaring renunciation of fruits as the highest
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: yasman nodvijate loko lokan nodvijate ca yah
verses.meaning: He by whom the world is not agitated and who cannot be agitated by the world, who is freed from joy, envy, fear and anxiety — he is dear to Me.
verses.context: Krishna describes the devotee who neither disturbs others nor is disturbed by the world
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: samah satrau ca mitre ca tatha manapamanayoh
verses.meaning: He who is the same to foe and friend, and also in honour and dishonour; who is the same in cold and heat, in pleasure and pain; who is free from attachment...
verses.context: Krishna continues describing the qualities of the devotee who is dear to Him
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: ksetrajnam capi mam viddhi sarva-ksetresu bharata
verses.meaning: Do thou also know Me as the Knower of the Field in all fields, O Bharata. Knowledge of both the field and the knower of the field is considered by Me to be true knowledge.
verses.context: Krishna declares Himself the knower of all fields (bodies) and defines true knowledge
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: jneyam yat tat pravaksyami yaj jnatvamrtam asnute
verses.meaning: I will declare that which has to be known, knowing which one attains to immortality — the beginningless Supreme Brahman, called neither being nor non-being.
verses.context: Krishna begins to describe the Supreme Brahman that is the ultimate object of knowledge
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: bahir antas ca bhutanam acaram caram eva ca
verses.meaning: Without and within all beings, the unmoving and also the moving; because of His subtlety, unknowable; and near and far away is That.
verses.context: Krishna describes the all-pervading nature of Brahman, present everywhere yet beyond perception
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: upadrastanumanta ca bharta bhokta mahesvarah
verses.meaning: The Supreme Soul in this body is also called the witness, the permitter, the supporter, the enjoyer, the great Lord and the Supreme Self.
verses.context: Krishna describes the various roles of the Supreme Soul residing within the body
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: samam sarvesu bhutesu tisthantam paramesvaram
verses.meaning: He sees who sees the Supreme Lord existing equally in all beings, the imperishable within the perishable.
verses.context: Krishna defines true seeing as perceiving the Lord equally in all beings
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: yatha prakasayaty ekah krtsnam lokam imam ravih
verses.meaning: Just as the one sun illumines the whole world, so also the Lord of the Field illumines the whole field, O Bharata.
verses.context: Krishna uses the sun analogy to explain how one Self illumines the entire body
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: sattvam rajas tama iti gunah prakriti-sambhavah
verses.meaning: Purity (sattva), passion (rajas) and inertia (tamas) — these qualities, O mighty-armed, born of prakriti (nature), bind fast in the body the embodied, the indestructible.
verses.context: Krishna introduces the three gunas (qualities of nature) that bind the soul to the body
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: tatra sattvam nirmalatvat prakasam anamayam
verses.meaning: Of these, sattva, being stainless, is luminous and healthy. It binds by attachment to happiness and by attachment to knowledge, O sinless one.
verses.context: Krishna describes the nature of sattva guna — pure, illuminating, but still binding
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: rajas tamas cabhibhuya sattvam bhavati bharata
verses.meaning: Now sattva prevails, O Bharata, having overpowered rajas and tamas; now rajas, having overpowered sattva and tamas; and now tamas, having overpowered sattva and rajas.
verses.context: Krishna explains how the three gunas constantly compete for dominance within a person
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: prakasam ca pravrttim ca moham eva ca pandava
verses.meaning: He who does not hate illumination, attachment and delusion when they are present, nor longs for them when they are absent...
verses.context: Krishna describes the one who has transcended the three gunas
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: mam ca yo vyabhicarena bhakti-yogena sevate
verses.meaning: He who serves Me with unswerving devotion, he, crossing beyond the qualities, is fit for becoming Brahman.
verses.context: Krishna declares that unwavering bhakti yoga is the means to transcend the three gunas
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: brahmano hi pratisthaham amrtasyavyayasya ca
verses.meaning: For I am the abode of Brahman, the immortal and the immutable, of everlasting dharma and of absolute bliss.
verses.context: Krishna reveals Himself as the foundation of the imperishable Brahman and eternal bliss
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: urdhva-mulam adhah-sakham asvattham prahur avyayam
verses.meaning: They speak of an imperishable Ashvattha tree having its root above and branches below, whose leaves are the Vedas. He who knows it is a knower of the Vedas.
verses.context: Krishna describes the cosmic tree of material existence with its roots in the Supreme
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: na tad bhasayate suryo na sasanko na pavakah
verses.meaning: Neither the sun nor the moon nor fire illumines That. That is My supreme abode, to which having gone none returns.
verses.context: Krishna describes His supreme abode which is self-luminous and beyond material illumination
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: gam avisya ca bhutani dharayamy aham ojasa
verses.meaning: Entering the earth I support all beings by My energy; and becoming the watery moon I nourish all the herbs.
verses.context: Krishna reveals how He sustains all life through His divine energy
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: sarvasya caham hrdi sannivisto mattah smrtir jnanam apohanam ca
verses.meaning: And I am seated in the hearts of all. From Me come memory, knowledge and their removal. I am verily that which has to be known by all the Vedas; I am indeed the author of the Vedanta and the knower of the Vedas.
verses.context: Krishna declares He resides in all hearts and is the ultimate goal of all Vedic knowledge
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: uttamah purusas tv anyah paramatmety udahrtah
verses.meaning: But distinct is the Supreme Purusha called the highest Self, the indestructible Lord, who pervading the three worlds sustains them.
verses.context: Krishna describes the Supreme Person who is beyond both the perishable and the imperishable
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: iti guhyatamam sastram idam uktam mayanagha
verses.meaning: Thus, this most secret science has been taught by Me, O sinless one. On knowing this, a man becomes wise, O Bharata, and all his duties are accomplished.
verses.context: Krishna concludes the chapter declaring this the most confidential teaching
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: abhayam sattva-samsuddhir jnana-yoga-vyavasthitih
verses.meaning: Fearlessness, purity of heart, steadfastness in the yoga of knowledge, almsgiving, control of the senses, sacrifice, study of the scriptures, austerity and straightforwardness...
verses.context: Krishna enumerates the divine qualities that lead to liberation
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: dambho darpo bhimanas ca krodhah parusyam eva ca
verses.meaning: Hypocrisy, arrogance, self-conceit, harshness and also anger and ignorance belong to one who is born for a demoniac estate, O Partha.
verses.context: Krishna describes the qualities of a demoniac nature
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: pravrttim ca nivrttim ca jana na vidur asurah
verses.meaning: The demoniac know not what to do and what to refrain from; neither purity nor right conduct nor truth is found in them.
verses.context: Krishna describes how those of demoniac nature lack discernment about right action
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: etair vimuktah kaunteya tamo-dvarais tribhir narah
verses.meaning: A man who is liberated from these three gates to darkness, O son of Kunti, practises what is good for him and thus goes to the supreme goal.
verses.context: Krishna explains that freedom from lust, anger and greed opens the path to the Supreme
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: tasmac chastram pramanam te karyakarya-vyavasthitau
verses.meaning: Therefore, let the scripture be the authority in determining what ought to be done and what ought not to be done. Having known what is said in the ordinance of the scriptures, thou shouldst act here.
verses.context: Krishna advises using scripture as the guide for right and wrong action
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: aharas tv api sarvasya tri-vidho bhavati priyah
verses.meaning: The food also which is dear to each is threefold, as also sacrifice, austerity and almsgiving. Hear thou the distinction of these.
verses.context: Krishna begins classifying food, sacrifice, austerity and charity according to the three gunas
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: deva-dvija-guru-prajna-pujanam saucam arjavam
verses.meaning: Worship of the gods, the twice-born, the teachers and the wise; purity, straightforwardness, celibacy and non-injury — these are called the austerity of the body.
verses.context: Krishna describes the physical austerities that purify the body
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: anudvega-karam vakyam satyam priya-hitam ca yat
verses.meaning: Speech which causes no excitement, which is truthful, pleasant and beneficial, and also the practice of the study of the Vedas — these are called the austerity of speech.
verses.context: Krishna describes the austerity of speech — words that are truthful, kind and beneficial
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: manah-prasadah saumyatvam maunam atma-vinigrahah
verses.meaning: Serenity of mind, good-heartedness, silence, self-control, purity of nature — this is called the mental austerity.
verses.context: Krishna describes the austerity of the mind — inner qualities of peace and self-control
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: datavyam iti yad danam diyate nupakarine
verses.meaning: That gift which is given to one who does nothing in return, knowing it to be a duty to give in a fit place, at a fit time, to a worthy person — that gift is held to be sattvic.
verses.context: Krishna defines sattvic charity — giving selflessly at the right time and place
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: kamyanam karmanam nyasam sannyasam kavayo viduh
verses.meaning: The sages understand sannyasa to be the renunciation of selfish actions; the wise declare the abandonment of the fruits of all actions as tyaga.
verses.context: Krishna distinguishes between renunciation of selfish actions and renunciation of fruits
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: yajna-dana-tapah-karma na tyajyam karyam eva tat
verses.meaning: Acts of sacrifice, charity and austerity should not be abandoned; they must indeed be performed. Sacrifice, charity and austerity are the purifiers of the wise.
verses.context: Krishna insists that spiritual duties should never be abandoned but performed without attachment
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: adhisthanam tatha karta karanam ca prthag-vidham
verses.meaning: The seat of action, the doer, the various senses, the many kinds of efforts, and the presiding divinity also, the fifth — these are the five causes of whatever action a man performs.
verses.context: Krishna explains the five factors that contribute to the accomplishment of every action
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: sarva-bhutesu yenaikam bhavam avyayam iksate
verses.meaning: That knowledge by which one sees the one indestructible Reality in all beings, not separate in all the separate beings — know thou that knowledge to be sattvic.
verses.context: Krishna defines sattvic knowledge as seeing one undivided reality in all beings
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: yat tad agre visam iva pariname mrtopamam
verses.meaning: That which is like poison at first but in the end like nectar — that happiness is declared to be sattvic, born of the purity of one's own mind due to Self-realisation.
verses.context: Krishna describes sattvic happiness — initially difficult but ultimately blissful
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: sva-karmana tam abhyarcya siddhim vindati manavah
verses.meaning: From whom all beings have evolved and by whom all this is pervaded — worshipping Him through one's own duty, man attains perfection.
verses.context: Krishna teaches that perfection is attained by worshipping God through one's own prescribed duty
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: saha-jam karma kaunteya sa-dosam api na tyajet
verses.meaning: One should not abandon, O son of Kunti, the duty to which one is born, though it is attended with evil; for all undertakings are enveloped by evil as fire by smoke.
verses.context: Krishna advises not to abandon one's natural duty even if it has imperfections
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: brahma-bhutah prasannatma na socati na kanksati
verses.meaning: Becoming Brahman, serene in the Self, he neither grieves nor desires. The same to all beings, he obtains supreme devotion to Me.
verses.context: Krishna describes the state of one who has realized Brahman and attained supreme devotion
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: sarva-karmany api sada kurvano mad-vyapasrayah
verses.meaning: Doing all actions always, taking refuge in Me, by My grace he obtains the eternal, indestructible state.
verses.context: Krishna promises that one who acts while taking refuge in Him attains the eternal state by divine grace
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: isvarah sarva-bhutanam hrd-dese rjuna tisthati
verses.meaning: The Lord dwells in the hearts of all beings, O Arjuna, causing all beings, by His illusive power, to revolve as if mounted on a machine.
verses.context: Krishna reveals that He resides in every heart, directing all beings through His maya
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: tam eva saranam gaccha sarva-bhavena bharata
verses.meaning: Fly unto Him for refuge with all thy being, O Bharata. By His grace thou shalt obtain supreme peace and the eternal abode.
verses.context: Krishna urges Arjuna to surrender completely and promises supreme peace through grace
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: ya imam paramam guhyam mad-bhaktesv abhidhasyati
verses.meaning: He who teaches this supreme secret to My devotees, showing the highest devotion to Me, shall without doubt come to Me.
verses.context: Krishna promises liberation to one who shares this sacred teaching with His devotees
verses.speaker: Krishna

verses.text: arjuna uvaca nasto mohah smrtir labdha tvat-prasadan mayacyuta
verses.meaning: Arjuna said: Destroyed is my delusion as I have gained my memory through Thy grace, O Achyuta. I am firm; my doubt is gone. I will act according to Thy word.
verses.context: Arjuna declares his delusion destroyed and his resolve restored by Krishna's grace
verses.speaker: Arjuna

verses.text: rajan samsmrtya samsmrtya samvadam imam adbhutam
verses.meaning: O King, remembering and remembering this wonderful and holy dialogue between Krishna and Arjuna, I rejoice again and again.
verses.context: Sanjaya expresses his joy at repeatedly recalling the sacred dialogue
verses.speaker: Sanjaya

verses.text: tac ca samsmrtya samsmrtya rupam aty-adbhutam hareh
verses.meaning: And remembering again and again that most wonderful form of Hari, great is my wonder, O King, and I rejoice again and again.
verses.context: Sanjaya recalls the awe-inspiring cosmic form of Krishna with continued amazement
verses.speaker: Sanjaya

verses.text: yatra yogesvarah krsno yatra partho dhanur-dharah
verses.meaning: Wherever is Krishna, the Lord of Yoga, wherever is Partha, the archer, there are prosperity, victory, happiness and firm policy; this is my conviction.
verses.context: Sanjaya concludes the Gita with the declaration that where Krishna and Arjuna are, there is all auspiciousness
verses.speaker: Sanjaya
